questionText: The time, $X$ minutes, taken by a worker to assemble a product is modelled by $X \sim N(\mu,\ 8^2)$, where $\mu$ is unknown. Records show that $15\%$ of workers take longer than $40$ minutes. Find the value of $\mu$, giving your answer to $3$ significant figures.

steps.description: State the distribution and translate the information
steps.explanation: Assembly times are normal with standard deviation $8$ minutes and unknown mean. ‘$15\%$ take longer than $40$’ gives the upper-tail probability $P(X>40)=0.15$.
Only $15\%$ of workers take longer than $40$ minutes, so more than half take less; the value $40$ therefore lies to the right of the mean, and we expect $\mu<40$.
The inverse-normal works from the lower tail, so we rewrite the statement: if $15\%$ exceed $40$, then $85\%$ fall below it.
The standard-normal value with $85\%$ of the area below it is $1.0364$, positive because $40$ lies above the mean.
Standardising $40$ gives this $z$; substituting $\sigma=8$ leaves $\mu$ as the only unknown.
Multiplying both sides by $8$ removes the denominator and gives a linear equation in $\mu$.
Isolating $\mu$ shows the mean is $40$ minus $8.291$; we subtract because $40$ lies above the mean.
Carrying out the subtraction gives the mean assembly time.

steps.description: State the answer to 3 significant figures
steps.explanation: To three significant figures the mean time is $31.7$ minutes, which is below $40$ as we predicted.

steps.description: Check the answer reproduces the given probability
steps.explanation: Standardising $40$ with this mean gives about $1.04$, whose upper-tail area is $0.15$, confirming the mean.
